core_mechanism: an obstruction/certificate argument in which the non-existence of a solution to a parameterized linear difference (telescoping) equation certifies algebraic independence — i.e., the unsolvability of a linear operator equation over a given function ring is used as a decision criterion proving that the objects are structurally independent/transcendent
brief_reason: The abstract states that the non-existence of a creative/parameterized telescoping solution proves algebraic independence of sums (and, with summation theory, transcendence), turning the absence of a solution to a linear telescoping equation into a certificate of independence.
